content: Hybrid storage: S3 primary + EFS for POSIX workspaces. EFS Access Points per tenant for isolation. Cost: ~/mo per 100 tenants, negligible vs LLM spend.

name: observability-stack-security-compliance
description: SNS alarm topics and CloudWatch log groups must use KMS encryption (platformKey from SecurityStack) for security compliance and multi-tenant data isolation

name: xray-tracing-group-service-filter
description: X-Ray tracing groups use filterExpression='service("chimera-*")' to aggregate traces across all microservices (ECS, Lambda, API Gateway) for end-to-end distributed tracing visibility

name: cloudwatch-dashboard-real-metrics
description: Replace placeholder TextWidgets with actual GraphWidgets using AWS service namespaces (AWS/ApiGateway, AWS/ECS, AWS/Lambda) for prod-ready monitoring dashboards

name: placeholder-implementation-pattern
description: Core package uses placeholder implementations for AWS SDK integration (AgentCore Runtime API, AgentCore Memory API). Allows TypeScript build/test now, actual AWS SDK integration later. Each method returns mock/error with 'not yet implemented' message.

name: type-collision-resolution-monorepo
description: When merging independent modules with type collisions (ToolCall, MemoryResult), prefix types with module name (MemoryToolCall, MemoryQueryResult) to maintain backward compatibility. Pattern: {Module}{OriginalType} naming avoids cascading refactors.

name: ecs-fargate-sse-streaming-gateway
description: ECS Fargate service for SSE streaming (Vercel AI SDK): ALB (public subnets) -> Fargate tasks (private subnets), auto-scaling on CPU/memory, IAM execution role (pull images/logs/secrets) separate from task role (DynamoDB/Bedrock/SecretsManager runtime access), placeholder Docker image replaced by CI/CD, 30s ALB deregistration delay for graceful shutdown.

name: skill-registry-naming-collision
description: When both tools/ and skills/ modules export SkillRegistry, use explicit re-export with alias (SkillRegistryService) in main index.ts to avoid TypeScript collision. tools/SkillRegistry is in-memory prototype with adapters, skills/SkillRegistry is DDB-backed production service.

name: skill-pipeline-step-functions
description: 7-stage security scanning pipeline for marketplace skills. Uses Step Functions Standard with Lambda tasks for: (1) Static Analysis, (2) Dependency Audit, (3) Sandbox Run, (4) Permission Validation, (5) Signing, (6) Monitoring Config, (7) Community Reporting. Choice states branch on stage results, .addCatch() handles errors, failure notification Lambda runs before ScanRejected termination. Enables clear audit trail and graceful failure recovery.

name: tsconfig-sourcemap-inheritance-conflict
description: When extending parent tsconfig.json, child configs must not redeclare conflicting compiler options. Parent with sourceMap:true conflicts with child inlineSourceMap:true (TS5053). Solution: remove inline* options from child, inherit parent's sourceMap. Applies to monorepo project references.

name: github-actions-cicd-workflows
description: Three-workflow CI/CD pipeline for Chimera: (1) ci.yml - PR validation with parallel fast-feedback jobs (lint/typecheck/tests/cdk-synth/security), (2) deploy.yml - Canary deployment with progressive rollout (5% -> 25% -> 50% -> 100%) and CloudWatch alarm monitoring, (3) security-scan.yml - Multi-layer security scanning (Trivy, Snyk, CodeQL, Semgrep, TruffleHog, cdk-nag, Checkov) aligned with OWASP LLM Top 10.

name: codepipeline-canary-orchestration
description: CodePipeline integrates with Step Functions for canary deployment orchestration. Pipeline stage invokes Step Functions state machine passing artifact metadata. State machine coordinates: (1) canary deploy (5% traffic), (2) 30-min bake with CloudWatch metric validation, (3) progressive rollout (25→50→100%) with wait states, (4) auto-rollback on validation failure. Lambda functions wrap Bedrock Agent Runtime API calls for traffic shifting. CloudWatch alarms trigger SNS notifications but Step Functions validates metrics programmatically during bake period.

name: codebuild-multi-stage-validation
description: CodeBuild project BuildSpec organizes CI checks as numbered stages in build phase: (1) lint + typecheck, (2) unit tests, (3) contract tests, (4) CDK synth + cdk-nag security validation, (5) Docker image build + ECR push. Each stage explicitly echoes stage name for CloudWatch Logs clarity. Artifacts include image-uri.txt for downstream stages. Build uses Docker-in-Docker (privileged: true) with layer caching. Bun package manager cache persisted across builds.

name: test-package-workspace-integration
description: Tests as workspace member: Add 'tests' to root workspaces array, update root tsconfig.json references, tests/tsconfig.json extends root and references packages. Enables @chimera/* imports and build-time type checking across test and production code.

name: tenant-agent-cron-workflow-upgrade
description: Phase 5 cron job upgrade: Replaced Pass states with 4-stage Step Functions workflow (LoadPrompt -> InvokeAgent -> WriteOutput -> PublishEvent). Each stage uses Lambda with tenant-scoped IAM permissions. Publishes completion events to EventBridge for observability.

name: evolution-stack-step-functions-pattern
description: EvolutionStack uses 4 Step Functions state machines (prompt evolution, skill generation, memory GC, feedback processing) with EventBridge scheduled triggers. Each pipeline has dedicated Lambda functions for tasks, CloudWatch log groups for tracing, and follows consistent error handling with Succeed/Fail states.

name: evolution-state-table-gsi-design
description: chimera-evolution-state table uses PK=TENANT#{id}, SK=type-specific keys (PROMPT_AB#{id}, MODEL_ROUTING, MEMORY#{agent}#{id}, FEEDBACK#{ts}#{uuid}). GSI1 for memory lifecycle queries (PK=TENANT#{id}#LIFECYCLE#{status}, SK=last_accessed). GSI2 for unprocessed feedback batch queries (PK=TENANT#{id}#UNPROCESSED, SK=feedback_type#timestamp). TTL attribute for auto-expiration.

name: cedar-policy-construct-tenant-onboarding
description: AWS Verified Permissions Cedar policy infrastructure integrated via L3 construct (CedarPolicyConstruct) and TenantOnboardingStack provides Step Functions workflow for provisioning tenant DDB records, Cognito groups, IAM roles, S3 prefixes, and Cedar policies with audit logging

name: cdk-stack-dependency-ordering
description: CDK stacks should be ordered by dependency depth (level 0, 1, 2, ...) not chronological addition. When parallel builders add stacks, reconciliation requires: (1) map dependency graph levels, (2) remove spurious addDependency() calls not backed by resource references, (3) reorder stack instantiation by level, (4) update numbering comments. SecurityStack having addDependency(networkStack) despite not using any Network outputs was a spurious dependency caught during reconciliation.

name: cdk-stepfunctions-state-reuse-error
description: Step Functions states cannot have next() called multiple times. Create chain once (e.g., failureChain = task.next(endState)), then reference that chain in multiple Choice branches. Error: 'State X already has a next state'.

name: cdk-construct-id-collision
description: Lambda Functions and Step Functions LambdaInvoke tasks in same stack scope need unique IDs. Pattern: Lambda = 'CreateTenantRecord', Task = 'CreateTenantRecordTask'. Prevents: 'There is already a Construct with name X' error.

content: Multi-region data residency enforcement: IAM SCP denies resource creation outside approved regions, tenant metadata includes dataRegion and dataResidencyPolicy tags (GDPR, HIPAA), no Global Tables for compliance-sensitive tenants (region-specific storage only)

agent-canary-deployment-orchestration: Step Functions orchestrates agent infrastructure deployment with progressive rollout (5%→25%→50%→100%), 30min canary bake, CloudWatch auto-rollback. Rollback bypasses canary (5min recovery vs 69min deployment). Cost /bin/zsh.07/deploy, 69min to full traffic.

local-dev-docker-compose-pattern: Docker Compose with DynamoDB Local (port 8000) + LocalStack (port 4566) + DynamoDB Admin UI (port 8001) provides AWS service parity for local development. Key services: dynamodb, s3, sqs, events, logs, iam, sts, kms, secretsmanager. Persistence enabled via named volumes.

name: infra-builder-cdk-generation-methods
description: Three CDK generation strategies: (1) Template-based for safe, known operations (scale_horizontal, update_env_var, etc.), (2) L3 construct composition for common patterns (data-lake-ingestion), (3) LLM-assisted for novel requirements with validation gates. Template-based is production-ready, LLM requires CDK synth validation and cost analysis.

name: cedar-provisioning-tier-quotas
description: Tier-based cost quotas for agent provisioning: basic ($500/month, $50 auto-approve), advanced ($2000/month, $100 auto-approve), enterprise ($10000/month, $200 auto-approve). Emergency self-healing bypasses cost limits for critical alarms.

name: drift-detector-source-classification
description: Infrastructure drift detector identifies change sources: (1) agent-caused (environment variables, task definitions - matches evolution logs), (2) external-caused (tags, descriptions - correlates with CloudTrail), (3) unknown. Safe drift (TaskDefinition, Lambda config) can auto-revert; dangerous drift (DynamoDB, S3, RDS) requires manual review.

name: infra-builder-module-structure
description: Infrastructure-as-capability module at packages/core/src/infra-builder/ follows established patterns: (1) types.ts with comprehensive type definitions, (2) separate manager classes (CodeCommitWorkspaceManager, CodePipelineDeployer), (3) InfraOperationResult wrapper for consistent error handling, (4) tenant-scoped operations via AWSToolContext, (5) AWS SDK client injection for testability.

content: cdk-synth-verification: Verify all CDK stacks synthesize with 'npx cdk synth --quiet' before closing Phase 0. All 11 stacks must successfully synthesize. Warnings about deprecated APIs are logged but don't block synthesis.

name: cicd-pipeline-test-stage-separation
description: CI/CD pipeline uses dedicated Test stage between Build and Deploy for integration/E2E validation. Build stage (buildspec.yml) runs fast tests (lint, typecheck, unit, contract) in <8min. Test stage runs slower integration and E2E tests against built Docker image before canary deployment. Separation enables fast feedback on code quality while preventing bad builds from reaching deployment stages.

content: CodeBuild buildspec should be external file (buildspec.yml at repo root) rather than embedded in CDK construct. External buildspec enables: (1) version control visibility of build changes, (2) easier local testing with AWS CodeBuild CLI, (3) separation of infrastructure (CDK) from build logic (buildspec). Reference with BuildSpec.fromSourceFilename('buildspec.yml') in PipelineStack.

name: codebuild-bun-installation
description: CodeBuild STANDARD_7_0 image does not include Bun runtime. Add install phase with 'curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash' and export PATH="$HOME/.bun/bin:$PATH" before pre_build phase. Required for monorepos using Bun as package manager.

name: github-actions-stepfunctions-canary
description: GitHub Actions canary deployment pattern: trigger AWS Step Functions state machine instead of manual Lambda alias updates. Use CloudFormation stack outputs to get state machine ARN, start-execution with imageUri payload, then poll describe-execution for SUCCEEDED/FAILED/RUNNING status. Avoids duplicating orchestration logic in workflow YAML.

content: CDK stack deployment in CI/CD: Use 'cdk deploy --all' with context variables instead of individual stack deploy commands. Ensures correct dependency order, reduces YAML complexity, and centralizes context passing (imageTag, envName) in single command.

name: severity-tiered-sns-alarm-routing
description: Production observability uses 3 SNS topics (critical -> PagerDuty, high -> Slack, medium -> email) for alert routing. Critical alarms (DDB throttles, API 5xx errors, cross-region failures) trigger immediate on-call escalation. High alarms (backup failures, cost anomalies) require 1-hour SLA. Medium alarms (4xx spikes, warnings) are monitoring-only.

name: cdk-orchestration-testing-pattern
description: CDK test suites use separate cdk.App instances per test describe block to avoid 'ConstructTreeModifiedAfterSynth' errors when testing prod vs dev configurations. Jest requires ts-jest preset and testEnvironment: node for TypeScript CDK tests.

content: eventbridge-scheduler-cfn-construct: EventBridge Scheduler uses CfnScheduleGroup (L1 construct) because L2 constructs are not yet available in aws-cdk-lib. CfnScheduleGroup only supports name property, not description.

name: ecr-lifecycle-rule-priority
description: ECR lifecycle rules with TagStatus.Any (maxImageCount) must have higher priority number than UNTAGGED rules. Lower priority number = higher precedence. Pattern: untagged=1, keep-last=2.

content: codecommit-self-editing-pipeline: Use CodeCommit (not GitHub/GitLab) as source for CI/CD pipelines that need to support self-editing infrastructure. Agent platform can modify CDK code through AWS SDK CodeCommit APIs (PutFile, CreateCommit) enabling autonomous infrastructure evolution.

CDK pipeline source must use CodeCommit (not GitHub CodeStar) per ADR-013. PipelineStack owns the ECR repository (chimera-agent-runtime) since it is consumed by the CI/CD build. cdk.json app command uses bun run (not npx ts-node) to align with project runtime. Dockerfile for agent runtime is at packages/agents/Dockerfile.

name: inline-lambda-cloudwatch-canary-validation
description: Canary validation Lambdas query CloudWatch metrics with get_metric_statistics() for error rates, P99 latency (ExtendedStatistics=['p99']), guardrail triggers, and eval scores. Calculate ratios from Sum statistics (errors/invocations * 100). Use 30-minute bake window with 5-minute periods. Validation thresholds: error_rate < 5%, p99_latency < 30s, guardrail_rate < 10%, eval_score >= 80.

name: rollback-snapshot-s3-restore
description: Rollback Lambda pattern: Store stable deployment metadata at s3://artifacts/deployments/latest-stable-metadata.json. On canary failure, retrieve snapshot, log rollback event to s3://artifacts/rollback-logs/{request_id}.json, revert to stable image URI. Include rollback metadata: failedImageUri, stableImageUri, previousDeploymentId, reason, timestamp.

content: lambda-inline-handler-implementation: CDK evolution-stack.ts and pipeline-stack.ts Lambda handlers use Code.fromInline() with self-contained Python. Critical handlers implemented: (1) detectPatterns - n-gram extraction with Counter/defaultdict for tool sequence analysis, (2) canaryBakeValidation - CloudWatch get_metric_statistics for error rate, P99 latency, guardrail rate, eval score, (3) rollbackChange - S3 snapshot retrieval + DynamoDB restore by type, (4) pipeline rollback - S3 stable image retrieval + audit logging.

github-actions-bun-monorepo-ci: Simplified CI workflow for Bun monorepos runs test + lint + typecheck in single job. Streamlined from multi-job pipelines (6 jobs -> 1) for faster feedback on PRs and main pushes. Eliminates unnecessary job dependencies and artifact passing.

name: bun-binary-compilation-matrix
description: GitHub Actions matrix strategy for compiling Bun binaries across 4 platforms (linux-x64, darwin-x64, darwin-arm64, windows-x64). Uses bun build --compile to create standalone executables. Handles Windows .exe extension via conditional rename step.

name: github-actions-matrix-multiplatform-build
description: Use GitHub Actions matrix strategy for parallel multi-platform binary builds. Each platform gets its own runner (ubuntu-latest, macos-13, macos-14, windows-latest) to compile native binaries. Matrix includes platform, os, target, and binary_name. Upload binaries to GitHub Release using actions/upload-release-asset.

content: bun-compile-cross-platform-matrix: Bun build --compile supports cross-compilation via --target flag (bun-linux-x64, bun-darwin-x64, bun-darwin-arm64, bun-windows-x64). GitHub Actions release workflow uses matrix strategy to compile on native runners per platform, then uploads artifacts for GitHub Release attachment.

cognito-hosted-ui-domain-pattern: Cognito hosted UI requires domain (cognitoDomain.domainPrefix = 'chimera-{env}-{account}') for OAuth flows. OAuth client config: generateSecret=false for PKCE, callbackUrls/logoutUrls for localhost + production, preventUserExistenceErrors=true for security. Stack outputs: HostedUIDomain, CognitoOAuthURL for frontend configuration.

codecommit-sdk-push-over-git: CLI deploy uses CreateCommitCommand API instead of shell git commands to send source to CodeCommit. This avoids credential helper requirements — the SDK uses existing AWS credentials. Pattern: read tracked files with git ls-files, build PutFileEntry[], call CreateCommitCommand. Handle empty repos by catching BranchDoesNotExistException (no parentCommitId needed).

name: codecommit-git-credential-helper-pattern
description: For large repo deploys (>6MB), use git with programmatically configured AWS CodeCommit credential helper instead of CreateCommitCommand API. Pattern: (1) git config credential.helper to 'aws codecommit credential-helper', (2) set UseHttpPath=true, (3) add remote, (4) send to remote, (5) clean up config in finally block. Handles repos of any size.

content: codecommit-credential-helper-deploy: CLI deploy uses git credential helper (aws codecommit credential-helper) instead of CreateCommitCommand API to send source to CodeCommit. CreateCommit API has 6MB payload limit — too small for large repos. Credential helper approach: configure git config --local credential.helper, add temporary remote, native git protocol send (no size limit), cleanup in finally block. Supersedes codecommit-sdk-push-over-git pattern (mx-82ace6).

name: optional-docker-bootstrap
description: Bootstrap scripts should make Docker optional, not required. Check Docker availability, log warning if absent, skip container-based stacks (ChatStack), deploy infra-only first. Users can add container stacks later. Pattern: conditional deployment based on prerequisite detection.

content: bootstrap-bunx-docker-optional: bootstrap.sh uses bunx cdk (devDep, not global), Docker is optional with graceful degradation — skips ChatStack and ECS verification when Docker unavailable. Stack names for selective CDK deploy use Chimera-ENVIRONMENT-StackName pattern.

name: codecommit-batched-createcommit
description: CLI deploy uses batched CreateCommit API (5MB/100 file chunks) instead of git credential helper. Eliminates git binary/pip dependencies, works with any AWS credential type (IAM roles, SSO, assumed roles). First commit creates branch, subsequent commits chain parentCommitId.

content: codecommit-batched-createcommit-deploy: CLI deploy uses batched CreateCommit API with 5MB batch limit (safe margin under 6MB API limit), max 100 files per batch, base64 size accounting (33% overhead), binary file detection (null bytes in first 8KB), and parentCommitId chaining between batches. findProjectRoot() replaces git rev-parse for zero-git-dependency deploy. Supersedes both codecommit-sdk-push-over-git (mx-82ace6) and codecommit-credential-helper-deploy (mx-2f4056).

name: codecommit-force-overwrite-pattern
description: CLI deploy deletes target branch before batched CreateCommit to enable idempotent re-deploys. DeleteBranchCommand + fresh CreateCommit sequence avoids API rejection when files unchanged from parent commit.

content: codecommit-force-deploy-branch-delete: CLI deploy deletes CodeCommit branch before batched CreateCommit upload. Fixes CreateCommit rejection when re-deploying unchanged files. DeleteBranchCommand removes the branch, then CreateCommit creates it fresh with no parentCommitId.

codecommit-no-changes-per-batch-handling: CLI deploy wraps each CreateCommitCommand in try/catch. If error message includes 'same as' or 'at least one change', the batch is skipped (no changes from parent) and parentCommitId is preserved for the next batch. This replaces the DeleteBranchCommand approach which fails because CodeCommit disallows deleting the default branch.

codebuild-cdk-deploy-iam: When CodeBuild runs cdk deploy, the build role needs sts:AssumeRole on arn:aws:iam::ACCOUNT:role/cdk-* for CDK bootstrap roles. CDK Pipelines adds this automatically but custom CodeBuild projects require explicit IAM policy.
